items.title: Sync Preview & Diff Viewer
items.description: Before any sync operation applies changes, show a rich diff of exactly what will change in each target harness — added rules, removed MCP servers, modified permissions. Users fear destructive syncs and often avoid automation because they don't know what will change. A preview mode with accept/reject per-item removes that anxiety and builds trust in auto-sync.

items.title: Reverse Sync (Pull from Target Harnesses)
items.description: Allow users to pull configuration from Codex, Gemini, or OpenCode back into Claude Code. Many users started on other harnesses and have well-tuned configs there. Currently HarnessSync is one-way only, forcing users to manually migrate their existing Codex AGENTS.md rules into CLAUDE.md before they can use HarnessSync. Reverse sync removes this migration barrier entirely.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles (Work / Personal / OSS)
items.description: Let users define multiple named sync profiles — e.g., 'work' syncs to corporate Codex account with strict rules, 'personal' syncs to personal accounts with relaxed permissions, 'oss' syncs only public-safe config. Users constantly context-switch between work and personal projects and must manually adjust config. Profiles let them switch contexts with one command.

items.title: Auto-Detect Installed Harnesses
items.description: Scan the system at first run (and on demand) to detect which harnesses are installed — check PATH, known install locations, package managers. Then auto-configure sync targets without manual setup. Today users must manually configure each target, which is a painful onboarding step that causes many to give up before first sync.

items.title: Intelligent Config Conflict Resolution
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that conflict with incoming sync (e.g., user edited AGENTS.md directly), surface the conflict clearly and offer three-way merge options: keep local, keep source, or merge both. Currently HarnessSync overwrites, causing users to lose manual edits they made in other harnesses, which erodes trust in the tool.

items.title: Sync History Timeline & Rollback
items.description: A visual timeline of all sync operations with timestamps, what changed, and one-click rollback to any previous state per target. The /sync-rollback command exists but is opaque. Users who broke something want to quickly see 'what changed when' and restore to any known-good state, not just the last one.

items.title: Feature Compatibility Score per Harness
items.description: Show a compatibility percentage for each target harness — what percentage of your Claude Code config can be faithfully represented in Codex vs Gemini vs OpenCode. Surfaces which features are degraded, approximated, or dropped entirely. Users don't know what they're losing when they sync, leading to silent behavior differences when using non-CC harnesses.

items.title: Team Config Sync via Shared Repository
items.description: Allow teams to designate a shared git repo as the source of truth for harness config, with HarnessSync pulling team standards and merging with personal overrides. Teams waste hours ensuring all members have consistent harness configs. A team-managed config layer above personal config enables standardization without removing personal customization.

items.title: MCP Server Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP servers in your Claude Code config are supported by each target harness, which require URL translation, and which can't be transferred at all. Include version compatibility notes. MCP server sync is a black box today — users don't know if their tools will work in Gemini/Codex until they actually try and fail.

items.title: CI/CD Sync — GitHub Action & Pre-commit Hook
items.description: A GitHub Action that runs HarnessSync as part of CI, ensuring all contributors' harness configs are validated and consistent on every PR. A pre-commit hook variant that blocks commits if config is out of sync. Teams lose time to config drift discovered at code review. CI enforcement catches drift at the source.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Layer
items.description: Allow users to define harness-specific overrides that supplement (not replace) synced config — e.g., Codex-specific tools, Gemini-specific context files. Overlaid on top of the synced base. Users need to tune behavior per-harness for legitimate reasons (different capabilities, different team members use different harnesses) but today must choose between sync purity and per-harness customization.

items.title: Natural Language Config Editing (Claude-Powered)
items.description: '/sync edit: add a rule that Codex should always use TypeScript' — Claude interprets the request, modifies the appropriate source config, and immediately syncs. Users know what behavior they want but struggle with the syntax differences across config formats. Natural language as the universal config interface removes format knowledge as a barrier.

items.title: Sync Analytics & Drift Dashboard
items.description: Track sync frequency, time-to-sync after config change, number of items synced vs dropped, and config drift accumulation over time per harness. A visual dashboard surfaces trends. Users don't know if HarnessSync is actually keeping things in sync or silently falling behind. Metrics build confidence and surface problems before they become pain.

items.title: Shareable Config Snapshot (Gist / URL)
items.description: Export your entire Claude Code config as a shareable URL or GitHub Gist that others can import. HarnessSync translates it to their target harnesses automatically on import. Developers ask 'what's your Codex setup?' and share config manually. A one-click export+import flow makes config sharing as easy as sharing a link.

items.title: Rules Deduplication & Conflict Analysis
items.description: Analyze your CLAUDE.md and synced targets to find duplicate, conflicting, or redundant rules across harnesses. Surface rules that contradict each other or repeat with different wording. As configs grow organically, they accumulate noise. This analysis helps users clean up bloated configs and understand what rules are actually effective.

items.title: Plugin & Extension Sync Across Harnesses
items.description: Sync not just rules and config but also plugins and extensions — mapping Claude Code plugins to equivalent Codex/Gemini extensions where they exist, and noting gaps where no equivalent exists. Users install a great plugin in Claude Code and want equivalent functionality in other harnesses but don't know what to install.

items.title: Config Templates Marketplace
items.description: A community-driven library of Claude Code config templates (by language, framework, team size, domain) that users can browse and apply. HarnessSync handles the translation to all target harnesses. New users spend hours figuring out what rules to write. Templates give them a curated starting point that works across all their harnesses immediately.

items.title: Auto-Sync When New Harness Is Installed
items.description: Detect when a new harness (Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) is installed on the system and automatically run HarnessSync to configure it. The first use of any new harness currently requires manual sync setup. Auto-detection means the new tool is ready to use immediately with your full config, dramatically improving the new-harness experience.

items.title: Selective Sync — Choose What Goes Where
items.description: Fine-grained control over which config sections sync to which targets — e.g., 'sync MCP servers to Gemini but not Codex', 'sync rules to all but skills only to OpenCode'. Currently sync is all-or-nothing per target. Users have legitimate reasons to keep some config harness-specific (proprietary MCP servers, internal tools) that they can't expose to all targets.

items.title: Proactive Config Health Alerts
items.description: Notify users when synced harness config becomes stale (target was updated manually), when a harness binary update breaks config compatibility, or when MCP servers become unreachable. Today users discover problems when they try to use another harness and it fails. Proactive alerts catch issues before they become blocking problems.

items.title: Harness Update Impact Tracker
items.description: When Codex, Gemini, or OpenCode releases a new version, HarnessSync automatically checks if that update changes config compatibility and alerts the user with specific impacts — 'Gemini 2.1 dropped support for X config key you use'. Users find out about breaking changes when their synced config suddenly stops working. Proactive tracking prevents surprise.

items.title: Harness Migration Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that guides users through migrating from another harness to Claude Code as primary — importing their existing AGENTS.md / Gemini config into CLAUDE.md format, then setting up HarnessSync for ongoing sync. The reverse-migration path is completely unguided today, blocking adoption by users with existing harness investments.

items.title: Sync Dry-Run with Change Cost Estimate
items.description: Before syncing, show not just what will change but the estimated 'cost' — number of files rewritten, symlinks recreated, time estimate, and any irreversible operations. A dry-run mode that outputs a complete change plan without executing it. Users with large configs or frequent syncs want to avoid unnecessary churn on their file system.

items.title: Config Inheritance & Composition
items.description: Support config inheritance — a base config that multiple project-level configs extend, with overrides cascading down. E.g., a company base config + team config + personal config, all composited before sync. Today users copy-paste shared rules across projects. Inheritance means changing a shared rule propagates everywhere automatically.

items.title: Cross-Harness Config Search
items.description: Search for any rule, tool, skill, or MCP server across all harness configs simultaneously — showing where it exists, what form it takes in each harness, and whether the synced versions match the source. Users lose track of which rules exist where, especially in large configs. A global search surfaces config knowledge without manual file inspection.

items.title: Sync Event Webhooks & Notifications
items.description: Trigger webhooks or notifications (Slack, Discord, email) on sync events — successful sync, sync failures, config drift detected, new harness version available. Teams who rely on shared harness config want visibility when something changes without monitoring log files. Push notifications bring sync events into existing team communication flows.

items.title: Cloud Config Backup & Multi-Machine Sync
items.description: Backup Claude Code config to a cloud store (GitHub, iCloud, S3) and restore/sync it to a new machine automatically. When HarnessSync is installed on a new machine, it pulls the backed-up config and syncs to all detected harnesses. Developers lose hours reconfiguring harnesses on new machines or after OS reinstalls.

items.title: Per-Harness Usage Attribution
items.description: Track which harness the user actually ran commands in and how often, then surface insights like 'you use Gemini 70% of the time but 40% of your rules only exist in Claude Code'. Helps users understand where their investment is actually being consumed and prioritize which harnesses to optimize sync fidelity for.

items.title: Context Switch Alert — Show Config Differences on Harness Switch
items.description: When a user switches to a different harness (detected via shell activity or manual trigger), flash a summary of what's different in that harness's config vs Claude Code. 'In Codex: 3 rules missing, 2 MCP servers unavailable.' Behavior differences between harnesses are invisible today, causing confusion when results differ unexpectedly across tools.

items.title: Harness-Native Config Preview
items.description: Before syncing, render a preview of exactly what the target config file will look like in its native format — show the actual AGENTS.md content, the actual opencode.json, etc. Users who care about native format correctness can't verify what HarnessSync will produce without running it. Preview builds confidence and catches translation issues before they land.

items.title: Capability Gap Report — What You're Losing Per Harness
items.description: A detailed report showing exactly which Claude Code capabilities have no equivalent in each target harness — skills that can't be approximated, hooks that won't trigger, MCP tools that aren't supported. Ranked by impact. Users are often surprised when behavior differs in other harnesses. A gap report sets correct expectations before they encounter the gap in the field.

items.title: Sync Preview / Dry Run Mode
items.description: Before executing a sync, show a colored diff of every file that would be created, modified, or deleted across all target harnesses. Solves the 'I don't know what's about to change' anxiety that makes users reluctant to sync after big config refactors.

items.title: Team Config Profiles via Git Remote
items.description: Pull a shared CLAUDE.md / rules set from a remote Git URL and sync it to all local harnesses. Teams can maintain a canonical config repo and every developer runs /sync-pull to stay aligned. Solves the 'we have 10 devs with 10 different AGENTS.md files' drift problem.

items.title: Conflict Resolver for Pre-existing Target Configs
items.description: When a target harness already has its own AGENTS.md or rules, detect the conflict and present a three-way merge UI (keep target / keep source / merge interactively) instead of silently overwriting. Prevents HarnessSync from destroying handcrafted configs users already had.

items.title: Selective Category Sync Flags
items.description: Let users sync only specific categories: /sync --only rules,mcp or /sync --skip skills. Today the all-or-nothing approach blocks adoption when users want HarnessSync for MCP servers but not for their hand-tuned per-harness rules. Granular control unlocks partial adopters.

items.title: Drift Notifications with Auto-Alert
items.description: After any file-write in .claude/, automatically detect whether targets are now out of date and surface a terminal notification or status bar badge. Solves the 'I forgot to sync and Gemini CLI has been stale for three days' problem that defeats the whole purpose of the tool.

items.title: Interactive Harness Capability Matrix
items.description: A /sync-capabilities command that renders an ASCII or HTML table showing which features (MCP servers, env vars, tool permissions, skills, hooks) each target harness actually supports. Helps users understand what gets translated vs silently dropped — currently a black box.

items.title: Named Config Snapshots
items.description: Let users tag the current state: /sync-snapshot save 'before-project-x-refactor'. Snapshots are stored locally and can be diffed or restored by name. The existing /sync-rollback only goes one step back — named snapshots let users experiment boldly and always have a named checkpoint.

items.title: MCP Reachability Auto-Fix Suggestions
items.description: When /sync-health reports an MCP server unreachable, suggest concrete fixes: check if the binary is in PATH, validate the port, offer to remove the broken entry from targets. Today it just reports red — users don't know what to do next.

items.title: New Harness Onboarding Wizard
items.description: A guided /sync-setup flow that detects which harnesses are installed, shows what would be synced, asks about conflicts, and runs the first sync — all in one interactive session. Reduces the time-to-first-sync from 'read the README' to under 2 minutes.

items.title: Secret / Env Var Masking in Synced Configs
items.description: When syncing env vars or MCP server configs that contain API keys, automatically replace secret values with ${ENV_VAR_NAME} placeholders in target files and add the real values to .env files (gitignored). Prevents accidental credential leaks when users commit synced configs.

items.title: Sync Analytics Dashboard
items.description: Track what changed in each sync: which rules were updated, which skills were added, which harnesses drifted. Surface trends like 'MCP servers change 3x per week' or 'Codex rules haven't drifted in 30 days'. Helps users understand their own config evolution.

items.title: Per-Harness Rule Override Annotations
items.description: Let users annotate sections of CLAUDE.md with <!-- harness:codex --> markers to include/exclude content for specific targets. Solves the case where one rule makes sense for Claude Code but is confusing or invalid in Gemini CLI format — without needing separate files.

items.title: Community Config Template Gallery
items.description: A curated set of starter CLAUDE.md + rules templates for common workflows (Python backend, React frontend, data science, DevOps) that users can install with /sync-template install python-backend. Solves the blank-page problem for new Claude Code users who don't know what to put in CLAUDE.md.

items.title: Config Dependency Graph Visualizer
items.description: Show which skills reference which agents, which hooks trigger which commands, and how MCP servers are used across rules. Surfaced via /sync-map --full as a Mermaid or ASCII graph. Helps users understand blast radius before deleting a skill that something else depends on.

items.title: GitHub Actions / CI Sync Integration
items.description: A generated GitHub Actions workflow that runs HarnessSync on every push to main and commits updated target configs back to the repo. Teams can enforce that all harness configs are always in sync as part of their CI pipeline.

items.title: Watch Mode Daemon with System Tray Status
items.description: A background daemon that watches .claude/ for changes and syncs immediately, with a minimal status indicator (menu bar icon on macOS or terminal notifier). Removes the need for users to remember to run /sync — zero-friction always-on sync.

items.title: Config Lint with Auto-Fix
items.description: Extend /sync-lint beyond reporting to actually fix common issues: trailing whitespace in CLAUDE.md, malformed YAML in opencode.json, missing required fields in plugin manifests. /sync-lint --fix applies safe corrections automatically.

items.title: Parity Score with Trend Tracking
items.description: Assign each target harness a numeric parity score (0-100%) based on how much of the source config is faithfully represented. Track this score over time and surface regressions. Users can see 'Codex went from 94% to 71% after yesterday's sync' and investigate why.

items.title: MCP Server Auto-Discovery Across Harnesses
items.description: Scan all harness configs for MCP server definitions (not just Claude Code) and consolidate them into a unified server registry. If Cursor has an MCP server that Claude Code doesn't know about, prompt the user to add it. Bridges the gap for users who configure MCP servers in multiple places.

items.title: Skill Compatibility Pre-flight Checker
items.description: Before syncing skills to a target harness, validate that the skill's tool calls, frontmatter fields, and referenced commands are supported by that harness. Show a compatibility report instead of syncing a skill that will silently fail in Gemini CLI.

items.title: Multi-Project Sync Orchestration
items.description: A single command to sync configs across all local Claude Code projects at once, with per-project override support. Power users with 10+ projects spend time running /sync in each one manually — this batches it with a summary of what changed where.

items.title: Harness Migration Assistant
items.description: An interactive wizard to migrate a user's primary harness FROM another tool (e.g., Cursor) TO Claude Code, importing existing rules and MCP configs and reformatting them as proper CLAUDE.md sections. Solves the chicken-and-egg problem for users who want to adopt HarnessSync but don't start in Claude Code.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, append a human-readable entry to SYNC_CHANGELOG.md describing what changed (e.g., 'Added 3 MCP servers to Codex, updated Gemini rules from 12 → 15 lines'). Gives teams an audit trail of config evolution without manual documentation.

items.title: Rule Tagging for Selective Target Routing
items.description: Let users tag rules with @target:codex or @exclude:gemini inline in CLAUDE.md. HarnessSync respects these tags during sync, routing content to appropriate targets. Solves the case where a rule mentions Claude-specific behavior and should never appear in competitor harnesses.

items.title: Expose Sync as MCP Tool for Agent-Driven Sync
items.description: Make the sync engine available as an MCP tool so other agents (not just Claude Code) can programmatically trigger syncs, check parity, or query the harness registry. Unlocks orchestration scenarios where an automation agent keeps configs aligned without human intervention.

items.title: Config Quality Scoring with Recommendations
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md and rules for quality signals: length, specificity, redundancy, missing sections (no permissions block, no style section). Surface a score and actionable recommendations. Users often don't know their configs are thin or contradictory until they see an AI-generated quality report.

items.title: Rollback Preview Before Restoring
items.description: Before executing /sync-rollback, show a diff of what will be restored vs the current state. Today users have to trust that the previous state was better — a preview lets them confirm before committing, reducing fear of rolling back to something worse.

items.title: Encrypted Config Values at Rest
items.description: Allow users to mark specific config values as sensitive, encrypting them in synced target files using a local key. Synced configs can safely be committed to shared repos without exposing tokens, connection strings, or internal URLs.

items.title: Auto-Detect Newly Installed Harnesses
items.description: Background check (or on startup) that detects when a new AI harness is installed (Cursor, Aider, Windsurf) and proactively offers to sync to it. Removes the need for users to re-run /sync-setup every time they try a new tool.

items.title: Config Split / Merge Refactor Tool
items.description: A /sync-refactor command that helps users restructure large CLAUDE.md files by extracting sections into separate rule files, or consolidating scattered rules into CLAUDE.md. Reduces friction for users whose config has grown organically and become hard to maintain.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Alerts
items.description: Track known breaking changes in Codex/Gemini/OpenCode CLI versions and alert users when their synced config uses a field or format that the installed version no longer supports. Prevents silent failures after harness updates.

items.title: Sync Scope Estimator (Files + Risk Score)
items.description: Before syncing, show: N files to write, N symlinks to create, N files to delete, and a risk score based on how many pre-existing files would be overwritten. Helps users calibrate how risky the pending sync operation is before pulling the trigger.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
